name: EthOutputRawPacket
title: EthOutputRawPacket
description: long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, dword fcs, char packetData );
long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, dword fcs, byte packetData );
long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, dword fcs, struct* packetDataStruct );
tables: long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, char packetData );
long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, dword fcs, char packetData );
long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, byte packetData );
long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, dword fcs, byte packetData );
long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, struct* packetDataStruct );
long EthOutputRawPacket( long packetLength, dword fcs, struct* packetDataStruct );

The function sends a raw Ethernet packet. The packet data must contain the destination (byte 0..5) and source (byte 6..11) MAC address and the Ethernet type (byte 12..13 in Motorola format). After that the user data are contained.
packetLength size of the Ethernet packet in byte (see Packet Length)
packetData packet data as byte or char array including destination and source MAC address, Ethernet type and user data (without checksum)
packetDataStruct CAPL structure containing the packet data

Example BYTE rawData[18] = { 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0xFF, 0x02, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x01, 0xF1, 0x23, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04 };EthOutputRawPacket( elCount(rawData), rawData ); //with calculated fcsEthOutputRawPacket(elCount(rawData), 4711, rawData); //with user-defined fcs
